results.title: SAST: Stop running commands one by one yourself | Product Hunt
results.content: # SAST. ## Stop running commands one by one yourself. Tired of running scattered scripts one by one to set up a project or complete routine tasks, manually adjusting paths and parameters every single time? This is the solution you've been waiting for. Launch tags:Productivity•Open Source•Developer Tools. No reviews yetBe the first to leave a review for SAST. AI that thinks + acts across your stack. 👋 I'm Leon, and I got tired of executing random scripts to handle database migrations, firewall logins, and spinning up microservices at my day job. No devcontainers, no automation - just pure manual chaos. SAST was born from that frustration. It automates command sequences, eliminates copy-paste errors with placeholders, and chains tasks into workflows/tasksets. My colleagues have been using it for months and love it. If you're stuck in the same manual grind, I hope this helps you too! Happy to answer any questions. This is an awesome project.

results.title: Container Diet: Slim down your Docker images with AI-powered sass. 🐳💅 | Product Hunt
results.content: # Container Diet. ## Slim down your Docker images with AI-powered sass. ### Trending products. Since the initial launch of Container Diet, the CLI has gone through a serious evolution. Pass **--auto-fix** and the Container Dietician writes you a complete, optimized **Dockerfile.diet**on the spot — works even without a source Dockerfile by reverse-engineering your image layers. **container-diet analyze --dockerfile Dockerfile --auto-fix**. - 🛠️ **--auto-fix** — generates a ready-to-use **Dockerfile.diet** with all fixes applied. I got tired of reading optimization advice and then having to manually apply it — so I made the AI just write the fixed Dockerfile for you. Can analyze your local images as well, layer by layer and main focus is layers, unnecessary packages or build tools, security risks, and multi-stage build suggestions. Curious: does Container Diet just analyze the final image, or can it actually suggest alternative base images / multi-stage setups too? Since it analyzes your Dockerfile (if provided) alongside the image layers, it specifically looks for:.
